recentReviews.author: holly ?
recentReviews.title: Really good
recentReviews.text:
Good app, easy to navigate and welcoming for beginner traders. However it takes far too long to withdraw funds from the app into other bank accounts. Really, you should be able to withdraw from the spending pot instantly into other bank accounts rather than moving money between pots and then waiting several business days for any money to actually be withdrawn. If it was a lump sum of money, fair enough, but it shouldn?t take a long time for something like ?40 to be withdrawn, especially to a bank account that i can make instant deposits from. I get it may act as an incentive to use the Trading 213 card itself but often I just want to disperse my money into my different savings accounts rather than actively spend it.
Otherwise its good!!!

recentReviews.author: Tradinglove4
recentReviews.title: Upcoming update of 19 march without any option
recentReviews.text:
Recently got an email that trading 212 is going to stop all active positions and open new positions when contract of USA Tech 100 is going to expire on 19 march. There is no option given to traders to continue with old system where we are allowed to continue with same positions and adjustment is made within the opened trade instead of closing the full trade and opening a new position. We are only goven an option that we can adjust that if we dont want to open new trade automatically which will furthur book more heavy losses.
There should be a 3rd option to continue with old system where positions are adjusted within the trade and trades are continued as it is like previous system when trades contract expires.

recentReviews.author: nyxworth_02
recentReviews.title: Worst Update
recentReviews.text:
Worst update ever. Seriously, please bring back the previous version. This new update makes trading unnecessarily frustrating.

You can no longer see the actual order price clearly because it keeps showing the constantly changing current market price instead. During volatile or peak trading times, when prices move quickly, the entire screen gets blocked by the closing order interface. That means you cannot even see where your order actually is while trying to close it.

In fast markets, every second matters. Instead of helping traders act quickly, this update makes it harder to understand what is happening with your position. You end up trading almost blind, unable to clearly see your entry or where the price currently sits relative to your order.

The previous version was simple, transparent, and functional. This one feels cluttered, confusing, and honestly like a step backwards for active traders. Please revert this change or at least give users the option to switch back to the old interface

recentReviews.author: Digital-Nomad1
recentReviews.title: Update ruined it
recentReviews.text: I'm honestly gutted and disappointed. This financial application used to be truly top-tier and a pleasure to use, but the latest update ruined it. It feels like you guys chose aesthetics and visuals over actual usability and simple, efficient function. Hiding the portfolio within a tiny swipe-up menu is just ridiculous?the font size is so small that I cannot read it! The previous version was absolutely perfect, and this new design feels like a massive and frustrating step backward. If the interface stays this clunky and difficult to navigate, I am seriously considering moving all of my funds today. I truly hope that you decide to revert these changes immediately... as it is so bad right now.

recentReviews.author: HeYhG699
recentReviews.title: The worst trading app
recentReviews.text: Gets worse with every single update - at first they completely ruined the way you buy CFDs, and now they don?t even show you what price you bought your positions at without clicking through twice, per position. They -NEVER- listen to feedback.
